agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module for an HR software system. Governs how employees accrue, request, consume, and carry over paid leave. The rules encode accrual mechanics, request submission constraints, approval workflow, and year-end forfeiture policy.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued leave days available to an employee, calculated as total accrued minus total consumed."},{"term":"Accrual","definition":"The process by which an employee earns 1.5 leave days for each month worked."},{"term":"Leave Request","definition":"A formal submission by an employee to take leave for a specified date range, subject to approval."},{"term":"Medical Leave","definition":"A category of leave that may be submitted retroactively (after the absence) provided a doctor's note is attached within 5 calendar days."},{"term":"Working Days","definition":"Business days excluding weekends and company-recognized holidays; used to measure the advance-notice requirement."},{"term":"Advance Notice Period","definition":"The minimum 3 working days between submission of a non-medical leave request and the first requested leave day."},{"term":"Contract End Date","definition":"The date on which an employee's current employment contract terminates; leave cannot extend beyond this date."},{"term":"Carry-Over Cap","definition":"The maximum of 10 days of unused leave that may roll into the next calendar year; any excess is forfeited."},{"term":"Forfeiture","definition":"The permanent loss of accrued leave days that exceed the 10-day carry-over cap at calendar year-end."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Employees accrue 1.5 days of leave per month worked.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Accrual rate is a policy parameter that HR leadership could adjust (e.g., change to 2.0 days/month for senior employees). It does not corrupt system state if changed.","testable_assertion":"After N complete months of employment within the current period, the employee's total accrued leave equals N × 1.5 days."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Leave balance must never go below zero. Leave cannot be taken before it is accrued.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A negative leave balance means the system has granted time off that was never earned — this is a data integrity violation. The balance is the core accounting invariant of the module.","testable_assertion":"For any employee at any point in time: leave_balance >= 0, where leave_balance = total_accrued - total_consumed."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Non-medical leave requests must be submitted at least 3 working days before the first requested leave day.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The advance-notice window is an operational policy. Management could shorten it to 2 days or extend to 5 without corrupting data.","testable_assertion":"A non-medical leave request submitted fewer than 3 working days before the start date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Medical leave may be submitted retroactively within 5 calendar days of the absence, provided a doctor's note is attached.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The retroactive window and documentation requirement are HR policy choices, not system integrity constraints.","testable_assertion":"A medical leave request submitted within 5 calendar days of the absence date AND accompanied by a doctor's note is accepted. A medical leave request without a doctor's note, or submitted after 5 calendar days, is rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Only a manager can approve or reject a leave request.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Authorization integrity — if non-managers could approve leave, the approval workflow is broken and audit trail is meaningless.","testable_assertion":"Leave request status transitions to approved/rejected only when the action is performed by a user with the manager role for the requesting employee."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Rejected leave does not consume leave balance.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Deducting balance for rejected leave would corrupt the accounting ledger. Balance must only decrease upon approved and consumed leave.","testable_assertion":"When a leave request is rejected, the employee's leave balance remains unchanged from its value before the request was submitted."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A leave request must not span across the employee's contract end date.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Granting leave beyond a contract end date means authorizing absence for a period the employee is no longer employed — a logical impossibility that corrupts scheduling and payroll.","testable_assertion":"A leave request where the last requested day is on or after the employee's contract end date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"At calendar year-end, unused leave above 10 days is forfeited. A maximum of 10 days carries over.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The carry-over cap is a policy parameter that could be changed (e.g., raised to 15 days or eliminated entirely) without corrupting data integrity.","testable_assertion":"On January 1, an employee's carried-over balance equals min(previous_year_end_balance, 10)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An employee has worked 4 complete months","when":"The system calculates their accrued leave","then":"The accrued leave equals 6.0 days (4 × 1.5)"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 2 days","when":"They submit a leave request for 3 days","then":"The request is rejected with an insufficient balance error and balance remains 2 days"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An employee submits a non-medical leave request","when":"The first leave day is 2 working days from the submission date","then":"The request is rejected for insufficient advance notice"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An employee submits a non-medical leave request","when":"The first leave day is exactly 3 working days from the submission date","then":"The request is accepted for processing (pending manager approval)"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An employee was absent 3 calendar days ago due to illness","when":"They submit a medical leave request with an attached doctor's note","then":"The retroactive request is accepted for processing"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An employee was absent 6 calendar days ago due to illness","when":"They submit a medical leave request with a doctor's note","then":"The request is rejected because it exceeds the 5-day retroactive window"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An employee submits a medical leave request within 5 days of absence","when":"No doctor's note is attached","then":"The request is rejected for missing required documentation"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A manager reviews a pending leave request","when":"The manager approves the request","then":"The employee's leave balance is reduced by the number of approved leave days"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A manager reviews a pending leave request","when":"The manager rejects the request","then":"The employee's leave balance remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An employee's contract ends on March 15","when":"They submit a leave request for March 13–16","then":"The request is rejected because it spans beyond the contract end date"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An employee has 14 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The calendar year rolls over","then":"Their balance on January 1 is 10 days and 4 days are forfeited"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"An employee has 8 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The calendar year rolls over","then":"Their balance on January 1 is 8 days (no forfeiture since balance ≤ 10)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Employee submits leave request on the exact contract end date for that same day","expected_behavior":"Request should be rejected — the contract end date is the termination boundary, and leave cannot span across it. Clarify whether the end date itself is a workable day or the first day of non-employment."},{"scenario":"Medical leave submitted on exactly the 5th calendar day after the absence","expected_behavior":"Should be accepted — 'within 5 days' includes the 5th day (boundary inclusive)."},{"scenario":"Leave request submitted on a Friday for the following Wednesday (3 calendar days but only 2 working days ahead)","expected_behavior":"Rejected — the 3-day rule is measured in working days, not calendar days."},{"scenario":"Employee accrues partial month (e.g., starts employment mid-month)","expected_behavior":"Spec says 'per month worked' — must define whether partial months accrue prorated leave (e.g., 0.75 for half a month) or require a complete month."},{"scenario":"Leave request is approved but then the employee's contract end date is moved earlier to fall within the approved leave period","expected_behavior":"Previously approved leave that now extends beyond the new contract end date must be revoked or shortened. The system needs a reconciliation mechanism."},{"scenario":"Year-end forfeiture race condition: employee submits leave request on Dec 31 for Jan 2","expected_behavior":"Forfeiture should be calculated after all Dec 31 transactions settle. The leave request for Jan 2 should deduct from the post-forfeiture January balance, not the December balance."},{"scenario":"Manager tries to approve leave but employee's balance was consumed by a concurrent approval","expected_behavior":"Approval must re-check balance at commit time. Optimistic concurrency control or locking needed to prevent double-spend of leave days."},{"scenario":"Doctor's note submitted separately after the medical leave request (within the 5-day window)","expected_behavior":"Spec implies the note must accompany the request. Clarify whether a two-step flow (request now, attach note later within window) is permitted."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Leave accrual creates a financial liability on the company's books. Incorrect balance tracking directly impacts payroll accuracy and financial reporting. The forfeiture rule caps year-end liability exposure.","operational":"The 3-day advance notice rule enables workforce planning and shift coverage. Medical leave retroactive submission balances employee welfare against operational predictability. Manager approval gates ensure team capacity is considered.","user":"Employees need a transparent, predictable system for planning time off. Retroactive medical leave with documentation reduces stress during illness. The carry-over cap incentivizes employees to use leave, promoting work-life balance.","risk":"Primary risk is balance corruption — double-deductions, negative balances, or missed forfeitures. Secondary risk is non-compliance with labor regulations that may mandate minimum accrual or carry-over rights depending on jurisdiction.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Employees","impact":"Directly affected by accrual rate, balance accuracy, request constraints, and forfeiture policy. Errors erode trust."},{"group":"Managers","impact":"Responsible for approval decisions. Need accurate visibility into team leave schedules and remaining balances."},{"group":"HR / People Operations","impact":"Own policy configuration, dispute resolution, and compliance. Need audit trails for all balance changes."},{"group":"Finance / Payroll","impact":"Leave balances are financial liabilities. Accrual and forfeiture directly affect payroll calculations and financial reporting."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Must ensure leave policies comply with local labor laws. Forfeiture rules may be restricted or prohibited in some jurisdictions."}]},"confidence":{"level":78,"reasoning":"The spec is well-structured with clear, testable rules. Most business rules are unambiguous and internally consistent. Confidence is not higher because several operational details are unspecified (partial month accrual, exact contract-end-date semantics, jurisdiction-specific legal constraints, and the doctor's note attachment timing).","high_confidence_areas":["Accrual rate mechanics (1.5 days/month)","Non-negative balance invariant","3-working-day advance notice for non-medical leave","Rejected leave does not consume balance","Year-end carry-over cap of 10 days","Contract end date boundary constraint"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether partial months of employment accrue prorated leave or require full month completion","Whether the contract end date itself is inclusive or exclusive for leave eligibility","Whether a doctor's note can be submitted after the initial medical leave request but still within the 5-day window","Jurisdiction-specific labor law constraints on forfeiture and minimum accrual","Definition of 'working days' — company-specific holiday calendar dependency","How concurrent approvals are handled when balance is contested"],"assumptions":["'Within 5 days' for medical leave is inclusive of the 5th day","'Working days' excludes weekends and company-recognized public holidays","Leave balance is tracked in half-day precision (since 1.5 days accrual produces 0.5 increments)","Forfeiture is calculated once at calendar year-end after all pending requests for the year are resolved","The contract end date is exclusive — the last working day is the day before the contract end date"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}

The rules encode accrual mechanics, request submission constraints, approval workflow, and year-end forfeiture policy.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued leave days available to an employee, calculated as total accrued minus total consumed.
